full_prompt: Design the primary lifting-arm structural yoke of a VTOL heavy-lift UAS defense-lift benchmark, targeting a payload-to-empty-weight ratio of >= 4.0 with VTOL-only operation. Target configuration: empty weight 100 kg, maximum payload 450 kg (ratio 4.5), MTOW 600 kg (including 50 kg fuel/battery). Lifting yoke is the structural beam that connects the vehicle fuselage to the payload hook via a central load pin at 1.0 m below the vehicle CG. The yoke must transmit 4410 N (450 kg x 9.81 m/s^2) vertical plus dynamic pendulum loads into the fuselage frame. Material: Titanium 6Al-4V STOA (Young's modulus 113.8 GPa, Poisson 0.342, yield 860 MPa, ultimate 950 MPa, density 4430 kg/m^3). Yoke envelope 800 mm width x 300 mm depth x 80 mm thickness, machined from billet with two 80 mm OD top attachment lugs (to fuselage carry-through) and one 50 mm central load pin bore at the bottom (to payload hook). Load cases per heavy-lift trial conditions: (LC1) static hover with max payload: 4410 N vertical on central load pin with top lugs reacted; (LC2) pendulum oscillation: 450 kg payload swinging +/- 20 deg at 0.7 Hz (natural period of 1 m tether), generating horizontal component up to 1560 N in both directions; (LC3) gust arrest: step gust encounter producing 3 g vertical at vehicle = 13,230 N through yoke for 200 ms; (LC4) snatch pick-up: zero-velocity pick-up of a 450 kg payload at zero-to-4 m/s in 500 ms = 3600 N additional dynamic = 8010 N total peak; (LC5) one-hook failure: asymmetric load 4410 N concentrated at one top lug (single-attachment fail-safe case); (LC6) fatigue: 5000 missions x (100 g cycle per mission) = 5x10^5 cycles of 0-4410 N. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises across LC1-LC5 <= 633 MPa (ultimate 950 / 1.5); (R2) at limit loads LC1-LC4, max stress <= 748 MPa (yield 860 / 1.15); (R3) pin-bore ovalization under LC1 <= 0.1 mm; (R4) fatigue alternating stress at fillet <= 160 MPa (Ti-6Al-4V Kt=3 endurance at 5x10^5); (R5) yoke mass <= 18 kg (contribution to 100 kg empty); (R6) first lateral bending mode of loaded yoke above 3 Hz (well above 0.7 Hz pendulum to avoid coupling). Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, fatigue summary, modal, mass report.
short_prompt:
Design the primary lifting-arm structural yoke of a VTOL heavy-lift UAS, transmitting payload load from a central 50 mm pin bore (payload hook, tether 1.0 m below vehicle CG) up into fuselage carry-through via two 80 mm OD top lugs. Target payload-to-empty-weight ratio >= 4.0 (empty 100 kg, payload 450 kg, MTOW 600 kg). Material class: Ti-6Al-4V STOA. No single governing airworthiness document is called out; design per MIL-HDBK-516C convention with ultimate FoS 1.5 and yield FoS 1.15, plus Ti-6Al-4V Kt=3 fillet fatigue.

Envelope: yoke on the order of 800 mm wide x 300 mm deep x 80 mm thick.

Load cases.
- LC1 static hover at max payload: 4410 N vertical down on the central pin.
- LC2 pendulum: 450 kg on 1 m tether swinging +/- 20 deg at 0.7 Hz, horizontal component up to 1560 N.
- LC3 gust arrest: 3 g step gust = 13,230 N through the yoke for 200 ms.
- LC4 snatch pick-up: zero to 4 m/s in 500 ms adds 3600 N dynamic = 8010 N total peak.
- LC5 one-hook failure: 4410 N concentrated at a single top lug.
- LC6 fatigue: 5000 missions x 100 g-cycles = 5e5 cycles of 0-4410 N.

prompt.title: Heavy-lift UAS lifting-arm structural yoke
prompt.brief: Titanium yoke transmitting 450 kg payload with 4.5x payload-to-weight ratio.
